title: Meet Math Puppys Friend Filmscratch Kitty
display_title: Meet Math Puppys Friend Filmscratch Kitty
wikitext:
[]: Meet Math Puppy's Friend, Filmscratch Kitty!             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Sep 21, 2007 10:46 pm I feel like I've read this somewhere, but I get deja vu very frequently so I have no idea. So anyways, I recently got Revival of Evangelion in the mail, and I was finally able to go through EoE's super-quick-flashing-images scene frame by frame. I noticed this: IT'S A CAT! IT'S A CAT! IT'S A CAT! It appears over four almost-consecutive frames, starting at the middle-bottom of the screen and exiting over the middle-left. This has no conceivable purpose that I can figure, so I'm guessing it's just there for the sake of doodling. So, uh... yeah. We now have a penguin, a dog, and a cat. The trifecta is now complete.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Sep 22, 2007 12:54 am Oops, I didn't mean to split the entire thread, just the selected posts. Too, bad you can't undo. SO LET'S TRY THIS AGAIN (and this time, less spam)

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sat Sep 22, 2007 12:59 am i didn't notice that befor. wow.

[]:             Quote Post by shinesman_cobalt » Sat Sep 22, 2007 1:02 am Was my comment about the Trigun cat spam? :( I didnt mean it! :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sat Sep 22, 2007 1:12 am what the heck is Misato doing in the background?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Sep 22, 2007 1:16 am TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: what the heck is Misato doing in the background?

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sat Sep 22, 2007 1:20 am *realizes* aaaaah! aha! tyvm. i remember now.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sat Sep 22, 2007 9:17 am So, Misato and a drawing of a cat - how are they connected? Hmmm.....

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Sep 22, 2007 10:54 am CitizenGeek wrote: So, Misato and a drawing of a cat - how are they connected? Hmmm.....

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sat Sep 22, 2007 3:34 pm (pulls out hair) STOP WRECKING MY MIND, ANNO!! Seriously, then i'm out of ideas.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Sat Sep 22, 2007 3:49 pm this is the same studio that put giant phallic symbols all over flcl just because the animators thought it was funny. Don't think to hard on this one im sure

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Sep 22, 2007 3:51 pm Isn't that cat image from one of the scenes in EoTV (Paraphrased: "Your image, not just your perception of the World, can change as well.")? Specifically, this? EDIT: Ack, okay, apparently, it wasn't that scene, but there IS some scene in EoTV that all these shapes are shown, one of them being two cats (they kinda look like the Magnet-Cats of Ritsuko's).

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sat Sep 22, 2007 3:52 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Isn't that cat image from one of the scenes in EoTV (Paraphrased: "Your image, not just your perpiction of the World, can change as well.")? Specifically, this?

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sat Sep 22, 2007 4:00 pm Me to. It was damn cool.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Sep 22, 2007 4:57 pm *Searches for the screencap she was mentioning* It IS part of that same scene I mentioned. The only reason the cats are all blurry is the scene goes so fast I couldn't screencap/pause it right. Found it from this Episode 26 Existentialism Clip here, http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Sp3fmAYbiO0 I dunno if there is a connection to the M26' Mindfrak sequence, but it wouldn't surprise me.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sun Sep 23, 2007 1:59 am good lord it's ritsuko's dead cats! AHHHHHH! and i love that part too. thought it was interesting about the cross-burning thingie.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sun Sep 23, 2007 3:23 am Actually, there may be some truth to that. In another thread, ages ago, it was theorised that the Ritsuko-dead-cat conversation was refering to Kaji being killed, in which case, the cat in this scene may make some sense.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sun Sep 23, 2007 8:57 am Indigo Arcangel wrote: Actually, there may be some truth to that. In another thread, ages ago, it was theorised that the Ritsuko-dead-cat conversation was refering to Kaji being killed, in which case, the cat in this scene may make some sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sun Sep 23, 2007 9:55 am I like to think that, even with the seemingly pointless religious symbolism. It may be an association know only in Anno's mind. For example, I associate christianity with morales, rules, "Thou shalt not kill", etc.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sun Sep 23, 2007 12:14 pm Indigo Arcangel wrote: Actually, there may be some truth to that. In another thread, ages ago, it was theorised that the Ritsuko-dead-cat conversation was refering to Kaji being killed, in which case, the cat in this scene may make some sense.
